paper.title_en: Sensing relativistic quantum fields with minimally perturbing local measurements
paper.abstract_en: We develop a framework for minimally perturbing local measurements in relativistic quantum field theory, with the aim to sense local properties of the field in a non-destructive manner. The field properties are sensed by weakly coupled pointers and encapsulated in conditional expectation values dependent on a postselection of the field state. Our operational protocol uses causally admissible Kraus updates for the field, in line with recent relativistic measurement theories, keeping in mind restrictions related to ``impossible measurements''. We illustrate our approach with three applications: a spacelikeness detector for causal-structure sensing, counting particle-creation densities in a supercritical potential and non-destructive discrimination between entangled states of the field and mixtures.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文构建了一个在相对论量子场论中进行最小扰动局域测量的框架，用于非破坏性地感知场的局域性质，并展示了因果结构探测等应用，与广义相对论基础高度相关。
decision.title_zh: 通过最小扰动局域测量感知相对论量子场
decision.abstract_zh: 我们开发了一个用于在相对论量子场论中进行最小扰动局域测量的框架，旨在以非破坏性的方式感知场的局域性质。场性质通过弱耦合指针感知，并封装在依赖于场态后选择的条件期望值中。我们的操作协议使用因果允许的 Kraus 更新来处理场，符合最近的相对论测量理论，并牢记与“不可能测量”相关的限制。我们通过三个应用来说明我们的方法：用于因果结构探测的类空间隔探测器、超临界势中粒子产生密度的计数，以及场纠缠态与混合态的非破坏性区分。
